error.notTokenError: The AGENT failed to load SKILL-related resources correctly. Please retrieve the default Prompt via the get_core_manual tool. Save the token ({{token}}) in the context for use in subsequent conversations.
error.toolAlreadyExistError: Tool {{workflowName}} already exists, please use another name.
error.downloadAssetsFail: Failed to download network resources, HTTP status code: {{status}}
error.fileNotExistError: Local file does not exist, please check if the path is correct: {{fileSource}}
error.getWorkflowFormatError: workflowPath must end with .json. The current workflowPath is: {{workflowPath}}
error.notFoundModel: Could not find model information for the corresponding type. Currently available model types are: {{models}}
error.hasExtendName: The current extension is: {{current}}, but the required extension is: {{need}}
error.notDir: Target path is not a directory: {{destinationDir}}

get_core_manual.title: Get Core Project Manual
get_core_manual.description: [System Guide] Core protocols and operation dictionary. Must be read first before initializing or calling other tools to obtain the latest parameter filling strategies and error recovery mechanisms.
get_core_manual.success: Core manual loaded. Please strictly follow its protocols and parameter strategies for subsequent operations.

get_workflows_catalog.title: Get Workflows Catalog
get_workflows_catalog.description: [Catalog Retrieval] Get the list of all workflows supported by the current server. Image generation commands must exactly match this list. Forging or guessing workflow names is strictly prohibited.

inputSchema.maxItems: Maximum number of items per page in paginated historical task queries.
inputSchema.token: The token obtained by executing the `generateToken.ts` script after the AGENT successfully loads the SKILL.
inputSchema.enableWorkflow: Whether to retrieve the user's workflow information.
inputSchema.useExistingCatalog: Whether to use the existing catalog file. If true, directly returns the workflow.json in the workflow directory, skipping the collection process.

get_workflows_catalog.success: Workflow catalog retrieved. Please strictly select the best match based on the results. Guessing IDs or names is prohibited.

get_workflow_API.title: Get Workflow Details
get_workflow_API.description: [Workflow API] Read the full underlying topology JSON of the target workflow. It is large in size and should only be called when execution exceptions require troubleshooting of the lowest-level logic. Strictly prohibited in routine tasks to prevent context pollution.

inputSchema.workflowPath: Target workflow name (prioritize the workflowPath field in the catalog; if unavailable, use the name field and append .json to it).

get_workflow_API.success: Workflow definition retrieved. Please use the returned JSON Schema to accurately parse parameter semantics and strictly abide by format constraints.

cui_get_task_detail.title: Get Task Details by prompt_id
cui_get_task_detail.description: Get task details based on prompt_id, including prompt, outputs, status, meta, etc.

inputSchema.promptId: The unique ID of a workflow execution task.

cui_get_task_detail.success: Task details retrieved. Please use the returned JSON Schema to accurately parse parameter semantics and strictly abide by format constraints.

mount_workflow.title: Mount Workflow
mount_workflow.description: [Parameter Mounting] Extract the supported interactive parameter Schema of the target generation task (hiding connection details). Before submitting a workflow task, this interface must be called to obtain a valid table of parameter keys.

inputSchema.promptId: The unique ID of a workflow execution task.

queue_prompt.title: Execute Workflow Task
queue_prompt.description: [Task Submission] Submit a task Prompt to the queue. The underlying layer automatically schedules computing nodes and synchronizes progress to the Host in real-time. Must ensure all keys have passed mount validation; fabricating keys is strictly prohibited.

inputSchema.isAsync: Whether to execute the task asynchronously. Default is false. Change to true only when explicitly requested by the user.
inputSchema.params: Collection of task parameter key-value pairs. Example: {'5_text': 'a cat'}. Must strictly follow the Schema.

queue_prompt.asyncSupplement: Asynchronous execution selected. Please ask the user to manually visit ComfyUI to check the results after a while. Conversation ended.

queue_custom_prompt.title: Execute Custom Workflow
queue_custom_prompt.description: [Advanced Mode] Submit a complete native ComfyUI API Prompt JSON directly to the queue. Open only for debugging underlying solutions or responding to explicit expert instructions; strictly prohibited for routine tasks.

inputSchema.apiJson: Complete ComfyUI Prompt format JSON string.
inputSchema.isAsync: Whether to execute the task asynchronously. Default is false. Change to true only when explicitly requested by the user.

queue_custom_prompt.success: Task ({{workflowName}}) successfully added to the task queue (ID: {{promptId}}). Please monitor the progress or wait for the results to be generated.

save_custom_workflow.title: Save Custom Workflow
save_custom_workflow.description: [Save Workflow] Save a custom parameterized workflow to the server's workflow directory, followed by automatic syntax validation and mounting. The submitted JSON must meet specifications (containing at least one ==Name== node that complies with mounting rules), otherwise it will be rejected.

inputSchema.filename: The filename to save as (e.g., my_workflow or my_workflow.json).
inputSchema.apiJson: Complete ComfyUI Prompt format JSON object (including node topology).

save_task_assets.title: Save Generated Assets
save_task_assets.description: [Save Generated Assets] Get the execution history of a specified task (prompt_id), download, and save all generated multimedia content (images, videos, GIFs, etc.) to the specified local directory.

inputSchema.destinationDir: Optional(Provided by the user; otherwise, the default value is used). The local path to save the files (if unspecified or invalid, defaults to the assets folder in the project directory).
inputSchema.overwrite: Whether to overwrite if the file already exists. Pass true to overwrite, default is false.

interrupt_prompt.title: Interrupt Task
interrupt_prompt.description: [Task Cancellation] Cancel the computing process of a specific `prompt_id` and forcefully remove it from the waiting queue.

inputSchema.promptId: The unique ID of a workflow execution task.

get_prompt_result.title: Get Task Result
get_prompt_result.description: [Output Snapshot and Assets] Obtain the node snapshot after a specific Prompt finishes execution, extract generated target media files (image/video links), or backtrack Traceback to diagnose errors.

inputSchema.promptId: The unique ID of a workflow execution task.

get_prompt_result.success: Task status retrieved (if data is empty, the task might still be processing). Please report the progress or display the final generated media resources based on the returned results.

get_system_status.title: Get System Status
get_system_status.description: [System Monitoring] Collect memory, VRAM, and Python runtime metrics for troubleshooting underlying anomalies like OOM or service deadlocks.
get_system_status.success: System status and environment diagnostic data retrieved. Please analyze the load situation or troubleshoot causes based on the returned metrics.

list_models.title: List Model Files
list_models.description: [Model Catalog] Poll the local disk model storage area. When parameters involve specific model files, this interface must be called beforehand for enumeration and calibration. Fabricating model filenames out of thin air is strictly prohibited.

inputSchema.typeName: Model type. E.g., checkpoints, loras, etc. Defaults to checkpoints.

list_models.success: List of available models retrieved. Please strictly reference the exact filenames returned when filling in parameters. Guessing is prohibited.
list_models.successButNoModel: Available model types exist, but there are no available models under this type. Please check if the model files are uploaded correctly or try other model types.

upload_assets.title: Upload ComfyUI Assets
upload_assets.description: [Upload File] Upload local files or network URLs to the input directory of the ComfyUI server so they can be applied directly in workflows.

inputSchema.fileSource: The local absolute path or network URL link (http/https) of the image.

upload_assets.success: Asset {{finalFileName}} successfully uploaded to the ComfyUI server.
